task.objective: Implement an installable Proofrail reusable GitHub Actions workflow that binds an AI-authored pull request to its exact commit, runs only base-authorized bounded verification commands, emits canonical Verification Receipts and an immutable Evidence Bundle through the existing deterministic kernel, enforces scope, review, and reported-check policy, renders actionable GitHub output, and provides the in-repository public prototype documentation and demo surface without changing canonical Verdict vocabulary or treating this Machine Task Contract as product runtime authority.

forbidden: modification or deletion of any existing Machine Task Contract
modification of any existing v1 Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, schema, release fixture, release golden, or exact-byte release authority artifact
canonical terminology, canonical Verdict vocabulary or precedence, Trust class, Evidence authority class, inference authority, or finalized-bundle immutability changes
private or parallel Verdict definitions in runner, orchestration, delivery, report, workflow, documentation, or site code

treating this Machine Task Contract, repository prose, a base-branch file, Builder output, model output, or GitHub metadata as Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, Evidence, a Verification Receipt, Verdict authority, trusted release authority, or external acceptance
MTC-based Policy selection, Evidence Contract selection, verification execution, Evidence creation, Evidence Requirement satisfaction, or Verdict influence
execution before exact base and head SHA binding, use of pull-request head configuration as authoritative policy, or publication after the current pull-request head differs from the verified head
unbounded command, timeout, output, environment, subprocess tree, filesystem, network, credential, artifact, diagnostic, or telemetry behavior
credential inspection, persistence, logging, forwarding to target commands, or artifact inclusion
secrets for fork pull requests, actions/checkout persisted credentials, privileged containers, self-hosted runner infrastructure, or secret-dependent verification
LLM or model execution, inference-based scoring, confidence, or natural-language judgment in the authoritative path
test deletion, skip, assertion weakening, validator bypass, architecture-guard bypass, CI failure allowance, stale generated output acceptance, or undocumented accepted failure
automatic merge, automatic deployment, GitHub App implementation, Marketplace publication, package publication, tag, release, billing, payment, paid runner, paid SaaS, or production deployment
GitLab, Bitbucket, Kubernetes, SSO, SCIM, SOC 2, enterprise dashboard, organization policy service, private runner, or cloud retention implementation

external demo repository creation or mutation, live external-repository verification, or live product GitHub API write beyond the repository-engineering non-force push and pull-request operations separately authorized by the user
force push, history rewrite, branch or tag deletion, merge, tag, release, package publication, or deployment

requirements: Treat these bytes as proposed content until external issuer github:gogun-rgb explicitly supplies the exact complete JSON bytes or their SHA-256 as PRODUCT-MARKET-001 through authorization event operator-event:product-market-001-public-prototype-v1.

Use exact baseline f643815f4acc716bcf817cb78636a9fa94ce7523 and materialize the externally supplied PRODUCT-MARKET-001 contract as the first task artifact and first local task commit after that baseline before any implementation or authority-bearing edit.

Treat this Machine Task Contract only as Proofrail repository-engineering authority. It must never become product runtime Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, Evidence, Verification Receipt, Verdict authority, trusted release authority, or external acceptance.

Preserve every existing v1 Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, product schema, reason-code identity, release fixture, release golden, and exact-byte hash. Add versioned v2 schemas and new market-prototype authority candidates instead of weakening or rewriting v1 artifacts.

Keep canonical Verdict values and precedence exactly ADMISSIBLE, REVISION_REQUIRED, REJECTED, and BLOCKED with BLOCKED greater than REJECTED greater than REVISION_REQUIRED greater than ADMISSIBLE. Treat PASS and FAIL only as verification result states. Represent missing evidence, stale target, system failure, scope denial, review gaps, and command failure through canonical Verdicts plus stable product reason codes, never parallel Verdict labels.

Add exactly one new domain-capability package, packages/verification-runner. Keep canonical receipt construction in that package and keep GitHub workflow orchestration, human rendering, artifact assembly, and delivery in the existing evidence-gate package. Do not add speculative receipt-builder, report-renderer, github-action, adapter, service, or framework packages.

Extend the architecture guard, package graph, product fixture coverage map, manifest inventory, and documentation for the exact seven-package surface and every new production import, subprocess, entry point, and trust boundary. Fail closed on unapproved edges and loader forms without weakening existing negative tests.

Add a closed market configuration schema and four deterministic presets named typescript-basic, ai-pr-strict, docs-only, and dependency-update. Parse the base-branch .proofrail/config.yml as untrusted candidate input, apply one selected preset deterministically, reject unknown or conflicting fields, and never use the pull-request head copy as authoritative policy.

Create exact new Trusted Configuration v2, Policy v2, and Evidence Contract v2 candidate artifacts with stable identities, versions, hashes, issuer lineage, authorization-event reference, target scope, execution boundary, required Observations, required Verification Receipts, Rules, and reason-code references. Keep them proposed until github:gogun-rgb separately supplies their exact complete bytes or SHA-256 values through an auditable product-runtime authorization event.

Permit verification execution only after the exact runtime authority candidates have crossed that separate external control boundary. If exact runtime authority is absent, stop authoritative execution with BLOCKED_RUNTIME_AUTHORITY as a repository-engineering status and do not fabricate a product Verdict or Receipt.

Implement a reusable .github/workflows/proofrail.yml installation surface that a caller can invoke with one workflow file, uses only pinned third-party action commit SHAs, checks out the pull-request exact head SHA with persist-credentials false, loads configuration from the exact base SHA, uses minimal token permissions, uploads a deterministic Evidence Bundle artifact, writes an actionable GitHub Step Summary, and returns exit code zero only for ADMISSIBLE in strict mode.

Do not require the consuming developer to install Node.js, pnpm, or GitHub CLI locally. Internal reusable-workflow setup may install the pinned Proofrail toolchain in its own runner workspace and must not expose its credentials or environment to target verification commands.

Bind repository identity, pull-request number, base SHA, and head SHA before checkout and command execution; verify the checkout HEAD equals the authorized head; re-read the current pull-request head after verification; return canonical BLOCKED with a stable stale-target reason and publish no admissible result when the head changed.

Execute only schema-valid commands selected from the accepted base configuration and applicable Policy. Use argument-array subprocess execution without an interpolated shell where feasible; otherwise make shell use explicit in the trusted execution boundary. Enforce per-command and total timeouts, terminate the process tree, bound stdout and stderr previews and bytes, compute full-stream digests, preserve exit codes, and fail closed on spawn, timeout, output, redaction, or receipt errors.

Construct the child environment from an allowlist. Remove GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN, ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN, NODE_OPTIONS, package-manager injection variables, and all unapproved ambient values. Fork pull requests receive no secrets. Target commands must not inherit checkout credentials or Proofrail control-plane tokens.

Redact secret-shaped values before any preview, summary, diagnostic, artifact, telemetry event, or retained receipt field. Record that redaction occurred without retaining the matched secret. Never include complete stdout or stderr in the Evidence Bundle.

Create strict canonical Verification Receipts bound to the exact target snapshot and command identity. Record schema version, stable receipt id, command name and configured command identity, start and end ordering data, duration, exit status or result state, stdout and stderr digests and bounded previews, timeout and truncation state, environment identity, runner and operating-system identity, Node version, dependency lockfile digest, execution boundary identity, redaction state, and lineage.

Extend contracts and the deterministic kernel so authorized Verification Receipts can satisfy applicable Evidence Requirements without bypassing Policy or Observation requirements. Receipts must remain authoritative only for the action, environment, and snapshot they record and must never directly assign a Verdict.

Preserve GitHub-reported checks as Observations distinct from Proofrail-executed Verification Receipts. Apply minimum approval, exact-head approval, changes-requested, reported-check, allowed-scope, denied-scope, and base-configuration-change Rules deterministically through trusted Policy and stable reason codes.

Use glob-based allowed and denied paths with deterministic cross-platform normalization and fail closed on absolute paths, traversal, invalid separators, empty patterns, ambiguous configuration, and denied-path matches. A pull request that changes .proofrail/config.yml must use the base version and emit a stable configuration-change reason.

Generate one immutable deterministic Evidence Bundle containing schema version, exact target, Trusted Configuration, Policy and Evidence Contract references, Observations, Verification Receipts, Evidence, Evidence Lineage, reason codes, canonical Verdict, scope, reviews, reported checks, human summary, every component digest, and an overall artifact digest. Use stable key and file ordering, LF bytes, bounded content, and no secrets.

Render the GitHub Step Summary and CLI output so a developer immediately sees the canonical Verdict, failed requirements, concrete reason and remediation, verified head SHA, per-command receipt state, scope violations, review/check gaps, and Evidence Bundle artifact name without needing internal reason-code knowledge. Detailed output must retain stable reason codes and non-guarantee boundaries.

Add deterministic local telemetry events for installation, configuration parsing, verification lifecycle, command duration, Verdict, reason codes, artifact failure, stale target, and rerun intent. Telemetry is opt-out, contains no source code or complete command output, performs no external network transmission under this contract, and is retained only inside the caller-controlled artifact.

Provide a browser-readable static landing prototype under site, a market-prototype demo fixture, copyable installation workflow and config, getting-started and troubleshooting guides, Verdict, Receipt, preset and Evidence Bundle guides, threat model, untrusted-PR and secret-handling guidance, pricing hypothesis, early-access and feedback paths, and explicit current non-guarantees. Do not claim Marketplace availability, cloud retention, payment automation, trusted release, enterprise certification, or complete sandboxing.

Add failing-first unit, integration, CLI, reusable-workflow contract, product-fixture, security, and static-site tests for success, command failure, timeout, excessive output, environment filtering, secret redaction, fork PR, exact checkout, changed head, base-versus-head config conflict, missing and stale approval, scope violation, reported-check failure, deterministic bundle equality, artifact generation, summary rendering, invalid configuration, and canonical Verdict mapping.

Run the real local CLI/action harness for a successful change and at least one failing adversarial case, capture sanitized transcripts and Evidence Bundle artifacts, perform browser visual QA for the static landing surface, and remove every temporary process, browser context, file, directory, and bound port with cleanup receipts.

Perform a fresh evidence-based second-pass review over the complete retained diff, original market-prototype requirements, contract scope, runtime authority separation, exact v1 bytes, new v2 authority candidates, test strength, protected paths, security boundaries, hidden scope expansion, and verification results without relying on Builder claims or model confidence.

After focused verification and full pnpm verify pass, create atomic local implementation commits without amending the contract commit. Repository-engineering non-force push and pull-request creation or update are allowed by the user's standing authorization; do not merge, tag, release, publish a package, create or mutate an external demo repository, deploy, or claim public launch under this contract.

requiredInvariants: The externally supplied PRODUCT-MARKET-001 contract is the first commit after exact baseline f643815f4acc716bcf817cb78636a9fa94ce7523 and every retained path is writable.
Every existing v1 authority artifact, schema, release fixture, release golden, digest, and accepted kernel compatibility case remains byte-identical and valid.
Canonical Verdict vocabulary and precedence, Trust semantics, Evidence authority classes, inference isolation, MTC/runtime authority separation, and finalized-bundle immutability remain unchanged.
No command executes without separately accepted exact Trusted Configuration v2, Policy v2, and Evidence Contract v2 authority and an exact bound base/head target.
The pull-request head configuration cannot weaken its own verification; base configuration, policy lineage, exact checkout, and post-run stale detection are observable in retained evidence.
Target commands receive no Proofrail control-plane token, checkout credential, secret, NODE_OPTIONS, package-manager injection value, or unapproved ambient environment variable.

Every Receipt, component artifact, and final Evidence Bundle is schema-valid, deterministic for normalized inputs, digest-bound, bounded, LF-normalized, secret-redacted, and tied to the exact target and execution boundary.
GitHub-reported checks remain Observations and cannot be relabeled as Proofrail Verification Receipts; Verification Receipts cannot directly assign a Verdict.
Only the exact seven-package graph and authorized production entry points exist; the architecture guard and product fixture coverage map fail closed on missing or unapproved surfaces.
No existing test or verification stage is deleted, skipped, weakened, bypassed, reordered away, converted to allowed failure, or made dependent on live credentials.

The implementation performs no merge, tag, release, package publication, Marketplace listing, external demo-repository mutation, production deployment, billing, paid service setup, or authoritative trusted-release decision.

requiredArtifacts: externally supplied PRODUCT-MARKET-001 Machine Task Contract committed first
versioned v2 Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, Verification Receipt, and market configuration schemas
separately supplied exact market-prototype runtime authority candidates with auditable lineage
bounded verification-runner package and canonical Receipt builder
receipt-aware contracts, kernel, trusted-config, release orchestration, and evidence-gate delivery
reusable GitHub Actions workflow with exact-head, base-config, minimal-permission, artifact, and summary behavior
four deterministic presets and market-prototype demo fixtures
scope, review, reported-check, configuration-change, stale-target, command, and system reason-code coverage
deterministic secret-redacted Evidence Bundle artifact and actionable human rendering
updated seven-package architecture guard and deterministic product fixture coverage
getting-started, product, security, troubleshooting, pricing, early-access, feedback, and static landing documentation
failing-first focused tests, full verification evidence, manual CLI/action QA, browser visual QA, cleanup receipts, and fresh evidence-based review
stopConditions: The exact complete PRODUCT-MARKET-001 bytes or SHA-256 have not been externally supplied through operator-event:product-market-001-public-prototype-v1: BLOCKED_AUTHORITY
The exact baseline differs from f643815f4acc716bcf817cb78636a9fa94ce7523 or contains overlapping unreviewed changes: BLOCKED_BASELINE
An authority-bearing target is required outside scope.write or any scope.read_only_authority or scope.forbidden target must change: BLOCKED_AUTHORITY
Canonical terminology, canonical Verdict vocabulary or precedence, Trust class, Evidence authority class, inference authority, or finalized-bundle immutability must change: BLOCKED_PRODUCT_IDENTITY

Exact v2 Trusted Configuration, Policy, and Evidence Contract candidates are required for authoritative execution but have not been separately externally supplied through an auditable product-runtime authorization event: BLOCKED_RUNTIME_AUTHORITY

A target command would require unbounded shell interpolation, secrets, persisted checkout credentials, privileged execution, self-hosted infrastructure, unmodeled network authority, or an environment escape: BLOCKED_EXECUTION_BOUNDARY

A required Receipt, bundle, redaction, timeout, output, environment, process-tree, digest, exact-head, or base-config guarantee cannot be made deterministic and fail-closed without weakening an existing invariant: BLOCKED_SECURITY_INTEGRITY
A new package beyond verification-runner, a framework, an external service, a database, a paid dependency, or an unreviewed dependency beyond the minimum YAML parser is required: BLOCKED_SCOPE
A test is weakened, a failure is hidden, a v1 exact byte drifts, a generated projection is manually edited, a protected path changes, or any required verification command fails: BLOCKED_VERIFICATION

Live external-repository execution, external demo-repository creation or mutation, Marketplace publication, package publication, merge, tag, release, billing, paid service, or production deployment is required: BLOCKED_EXTERNAL_AUTHORITY
A credential, secret value, API key, token, billing account, paid cloud, or paid SaaS would need to be requested, emitted, persisted, or fabricated: BLOCKED_CREDENTIAL_OR_COST_RISK
